definition.output: Approved, Published CSR and eCTD submission package
definition.customer: Health Authorities (HA), Ethics Committees (EC), Investigators, Patients
definition.valueAtom: Verified clinical trial evidence translated into a regulatory claim
definition.configurationNotes: Sequential, highly-gated waterfall process heavily dependent on upstream data locks, parallel drafting streams (Body, Appendices, Narratives), and cross-functional consensus. Highly fragmented across external vendors.
definition.trigger: Planned Study DB lock date available / Decision for need of CSR
definition.endpoint: CSR distributed, archived in TMF, and Study Disclosure performed

nodes.label: DB Lock & Data Cleaning
nodes.sublabel: Clean and lock clinical trial data
nodes.stateRationale: Highly compressed timeline (30 days) to clean up to 1000+ subjects. Intense pushing of site staff; complex external vendor data reconciliation.

valueNatures.description: Ensures 100% data quality before analysis begins

valueSpec.inputSummary: Raw EDC data, external lab/vendor data
valueSpec.valueAdded: Transforms raw, unverified data into a locked, trustworthy foundation for analysis.

nodes.label: SAP / IAP Generation
nodes.stateRationale: 90% of the analysis plan for standard studies is exactly the same, creating redundant inefficiencies and wasting 2-4 weeks.

valueNatures.description: Defines how the data will be analyzed and reported

valueSpec.valueAdded: Sets the statistical methodology framework.

nodes.label: Generate TLFs
nodes.stateRationale: External PK data arrives late; fragmented data sources from 11-12 different vendors. Produces 150-200 outputs.

valueNatures.description: Converts CDISC/ADaM data into human-readable evidence

valueSpec.valueAdded: Applies statistical methods to derive efficacy and safety results.

nodes.label: Define Report Type
nodes.sublabel: Decide CSR type and regulatory intent
nodes.stateRationale: Clear criteria based on protocol and regulatory strategy. Decisions are made quickly.

valueNatures.description: Aligns cross-functional strategy on CSR submission

valueSpec.valueAdded: Sets the scope and template framework for the entire CSR.

nodes.label: Build Route Map
nodes.stateRationale: Route Map disconnects from Veeva workflows, causing system audit gaps and out-of-sync tracking.

valueNatures.description: Establishes project schedule

nodes.label: Kick-off Meeting
nodes.sublabel: Align team on roles and timelines
nodes.stateRationale: Standard meeting to secure buy-in from all stakeholders.

valueNatures.description: Secures commitment to the Route Map

nodes.label: Setup RLCP
nodes.stateRationale: Intense manual effort to create placeholders and map 140+ documents. Template mismatch between internal structure and ICH E3.

valueNatures.description: Creates the document hierarchy in Veeva

valueSpec.valueAdded: Prepares Veeva for document ingesting and eCTD tracking.

nodes.label: Body Shell
nodes.sublabel: Create complete body shell with fixed text
nodes.stateRationale: Relatively straightforward adaptation of templates.

valueNatures.description: Sets structural outline of data-dependent sections

valueSpec.valueAdded: Frontloads methodological parts of the report before data arrives.

nodes.label: Review CSR Shell
nodes.stateRationale: Usually runs smoothly, establishing agreement on presentation.

valueNatures.description: Agrees on key data presentation and messaging

nodes.label: Results Review Meeting
nodes.stateRationale: Requires coordination of multiple fragmented data systems; late ad-hoc queries requested here severely pressure the timeline.

valueNatures.description: Solidifies the scientific narrative based on locked data

nodes.label: Body Draft 1
nodes.sublabel: Write CSR body and integrate TLF data
nodes.stateRationale: Highly error-prone manual copy-pasting of thousands of pages of data from RTFs/PDFs into Word. Style inconsistencies across multiple authors.

valueNatures.description: Translates TLF data into scientific narratives and claims

valueSpec.valueAdded: Synthesizes raw statistical output into regulatory-compliant scientific text.

nodes.label: Narratives Draft 1
nodes.stateRationale: Massive manual effort (100-300+ narratives per study) combining CIOMS forms and safety databases. Vendor outputs often lack consistency.

valueNatures.description: Creates patient-specific safety profiles

valueSpec.valueAdded: Extracts critical safety events into readable patient profiles.

nodes.label: Appendices Draft 1
nodes.stateRationale: Heavy dependency on external vendors (e.g., bioanalytical reports, subject PDFs) causing delays from weeks up to half a year.

valueNatures.description: Compiles supporting documentation

valueSpec.valueAdded: Ensures all regulatory required backups are attached.

nodes.label: Review CSR Draft 1
nodes.stateRationale: 24-30 reviewers. Reviewers fail to click 'complete' in Veeva, causing massive administrative drag.

valueNatures.description: Validates accuracy, completeness, and strategy fit

nodes.label: Comment Resolution 1
nodes.stateRationale: Structured meeting to handle conflicting feedback and coordinate revisions.

valueNatures.description: Harmonizes cross-functional discrepancies

nodes.label: Body Draft 2
nodes.stateRationale: Zombie references carried over from old templates; manual formatting consistency required.

valueNatures.description: Produces near-final CSR body

valueSpec.valueAdded: Applies scientific and strategic polish to the report.

nodes.label: Narratives Draft 2
nodes.stateRationale: Merging individual files or batches into a single MS Word document with hyperlinked ToC.

valueNatures.description: Consolidates narrative deliverables

valueSpec.valueAdded: Readies narratives for final QC.

nodes.label: Appendices Draft 2
nodes.stateRationale: Still waiting on late external data elements (e.g. PK, Bioanalytical) delaying the entire package.

valueNatures.description: Finalizes supporting appendix framework

valueSpec.valueAdded: Ensures all sections exist prior to QC.

nodes.label: Review CSR Draft 2
nodes.stateRationale: Final large-scale internal review before QC.

valueNatures.description: Secures broader organizational buy-in

nodes.label: Investigator Review
nodes.stateRationale: Required external clinical sign-off, unless blinded.

valueNatures.description: Validates clinical interpretation externally

nodes.label: QC - Body
nodes.sublabel: 100% check of data points against source
nodes.stateRationale: Due to manual transcription, every single number and claim must be manually checked against PDFs/RTFs.

valueNatures.description: Ensures absolute data integrity for Health Authorities

nodes.label: QC - Narratives & Appendices
nodes.sublabel: Format and consistency check on attachments
nodes.stateRationale: Standard procedural check.

valueNatures.description: Validates formatting and source matching

nodes.label: Approval & Signatures
nodes.stateRationale: Securing final signatures from CSR Approver, Signatory, and Investigator can cause delays due to system/access issues.

valueNatures.description: Formal organizational and clinical sign-off

nodes.label: RLCP Baselining
nodes.stateRationale: Veeva auto-matching logic floods the RLCP with up to 300 irrelevant documents when state changes to Baseline.

valueNatures.description: Locks the technical structure for eCTD publishing

nodes.label: Publish & Hyperlink
nodes.stateRationale: Any late changes force the document back to draft, breaking all manual hyperlinks which must be completely redone for 140+ docs.

valueNatures.description: Finalizes the eCTD-ready CSR package

valueSpec.valueAdded: Creates navigable, regulatory-compliant submission package.

nodes.label: Distribute CSR
nodes.stateRationale: Straightforward distribution via standard channels once package is complete.

valueNatures.description: Fulfills regulatory submission requirements

valueSpec.valueAdded: Moves the document from internal approval to regulatory assessment.

nodes.label: Archive in TMF
nodes.sublabel: File final package to Trial Master File
nodes.stateRationale: Standard operational wrap-up.

valueNatures.description: Secures the document of record

valueSpec.valueAdded: Ensures long-term audit readiness.

nodes.label: Layman Summary & Disclosure
nodes.stateRationale: Requires 10-12 hours of manual summarization of complex 400-page CSRs into 2-page layman documents.

valueNatures.description: Meets EU regulatory requirements for patient disclosure

valueSpec.valueAdded: Translates dense scientific data into accessible information for trial participants.

wasteSpec.driver: Strict 58-character limit and lack of auto-population in Veeva
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Automate POL generation based on study metadata

wasteSpec.driver: Lack of direct system integration, relying on Excel sheets and manual uploads
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Direct ingestion from binder ID or API

wasteSpec.driver: Merck uses an internal structure that differs from the standard ICH E3 used by external partners
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Harmonize templates globally to ICH E3

nodes.label: Redundant SAP Writing
nodes.sublabel: Writing full plans for standard studies

wasteSpec.driver: Governance processes require full documentation even for 'cookie-cutter' studies where 90% is identical
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Adopt modular or exempt SAP models for standard studies

wasteSpec.driver: Team identifies new data presentation needs during Results Review

nodes.label: Manual Copy-Paste Transcription
nodes.sublabel: Typing/copying data from PDFs/RTFs to Word

wasteSpec.driver: Lack of system integration between Analysis (Nexus) and Authoring (Word/Veeva)
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Live evidence linking / automated data-to-text generation

nodes.label: Vendor-to-Sponsor TLF Loops
nodes.sublabel: Reworking analysis files from external vendors

wasteSpec.driver: Outsourced data packages require internal mapping and quality checks
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Direct system integration with key vendor partners
Automated orchestrator linked to Route Map

wasteSpec.driver: Human error when using boilerplate templates across different studies
CRO EDC systems generating poor quality PDFs, blocking the CSR appendix completion
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: In-house generation capabilities or strict automated vendor QA

wasteSpec.driver: Late TLF changes invalidate previous manual copy-paste work
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Dynamic data-linking triggering targeted component re-QC

wasteSpec.driver: Veeva auto-matching logic based on broad study metadata
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Refine metadata tagging or automate removal of unlocked documents

nodes.label: Hyperlink Re-creation
nodes.sublabel: Redoing links after minor late changes

wasteSpec.driver: Static PDF/Word linking breaks upon version changes (draft to baseline reversion)
wasteSpec.eliminationCondition: Dynamic hyperlinking tool or component-based gating

nodes.label: Live Evidence Linking
nodes.sublabel: Dynamic data connection from Nexus to Word
nodes.stateRationale: Currently missing; relies entirely on human translation of static PDFs/RTFs.

valueNatures.description: Would automatically feed structured data into CSR templates

valueSpec.valueAdded: Eliminates copy-paste, ensures 100% traceability, speeds up QC.

nodes.label: AI Narrative Generator
nodes.stateRationale: Currently a massive 60-day manual effort by vendors; ripe for LLM automation.

valueNatures.description: Would generate standardized drafts from CIOMS/Safety data

valueSpec.valueAdded: Reduces manual writing effort by 50-80%.

nodes.label: Route Map Auto-Orchestrator
nodes.stateRationale: Currently MW acts as a secretary sending 24+ manual emails to chase reviewers.

valueNatures.description: Would sync Route Map with Veeva and auto-chase reviewers

valueSpec.valueAdded: Eliminates administrative drag on Medical Writers.

nodes.label: Protocol-to-Visuals Agent
nodes.sublabel: Generate visual study flowcharts from text
nodes.stateRationale: Currently MWs manually interpret protocol text to draw scheduling diagrams.

valueNatures.description: Reads protocol Schedule of Assessments to auto-generate graphics

valueSpec.valueAdded: Provides immediate visual context for reports, saving manual drawing time.

nodes.label: Layman Summarization Agent
nodes.stateRationale: Currently CTTR team spends 10-12 hours manually condensing a 400+ page CSR into a 2-page document.

valueNatures.description: Condenses highly technical language to 8th-grade reading level

valueSpec.valueAdded: Accelerates compliance tasks by providing a high-quality initial draft.

nodes.label: Data Cleaning Agent
nodes.sublabel: Automated clinical DB checks and querying
nodes.stateRationale: CDM faces massive stress trying to review 1000s of patients in 30 days before lock.

valueNatures.description: Flags logical data inconsistencies and drafts site queries

valueSpec.valueAdded: Relieves the primary bottleneck gating the entire CSR process.

flows.label: Data Cleaning Agent to DB Lock

forces.description: Data is trapped in static PDF/RTF formats from Nexus, requiring manual transcription into Veeva/Word.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'At the moment the entire writing is manual, so we really copy and paste... highly error prone.'

forces.description: Veeva auto-matching pulls all documents associated with a study number upon state change to Baseline.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'When you change the status... to baseline, then all documents are flying in... sometimes 300 documents... remove them all manually.'

forces.description: Strict 58-character limit for Published Output Location (POL) causes downstream failures if exceeded.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'If you cross 59... you'll have to redo the whole process just because you have missed a character.'

forces.description: Reviewers fail to click 'complete' in Veeva workflows, forcing MWs to manually chase them.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'We don't have a very strong culture here... we have to send multiple reminders to people to hit that button.'

forces.description: Massive volume of unstructured data needs to be manually synthesized into 100-300 narratives per study.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'Writing of 300 narratives or a big study that is quite time consuming... a human process.'

forces.description: Hyperlinks break entirely if documents are reverted from Baseline back to Draft state.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'When they change something in the study report... So we have to do all hyperlinks new because then nothing is working again... 140 documents... again.'

forces.description: Dependencies on up to 12 different external vendors for data (e.g. bioanalytical, subject PDFs) with varying standards.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'organize always have some missing samples somewhere... take sometimes three months, four months or even half a year.'

forces.description: Human error relying on copy-pasting previous documents carries over outdated or inappropriate text/references.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'sometimes the people use copy over an old reference... and it's not a study about animals.'

forces.description: Highly siloed data across Nexus, GoAnywhere, SharePoint, and Veeva creates permission bottlenecks.
forces.evidence: Quotes: 'Every system is handling the access separately... sharing of results or switching them from one system to another... is a pain point for us.'

frictionPoints.label: FP1
frictionPoints.title: The Manual Evidence Transcription Bottleneck
frictionPoints.convergence: Lack of system integration (F1) combined with late ad-hoc analysis requests forces MWs to manually copy-paste static data, resulting in massive QC overhead.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: Manual transcription creates errors -> Errors require 100% QC -> QC finds errors -> Manual updates are made -> Late statistical changes arrive -> Entire manual copy-paste + QC cycle must repeat.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Hundreds of MW and QC hours per CSR; high risk of data inconsistency in regulatory submissions.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Implement machine-readable integration layer between Nexus and Veeva/Word (e.g., Yseop).

frictionPoints.label: FP2
frictionPoints.title: RLCP Baseline & Hyperlink Collapse
frictionPoints.convergence: Veeva auto-matching rules (F2) and fragile hyperlink structures (F6) mean any late document change requires reverting the entire plan, triggering an avalanche of irrelevant documents and breaking all links.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: State changed to Baseline -> Irrelevant docs flood in -> Manual cleanup -> Minor revision needed -> Revert to Draft -> Fix revision -> State changed to Baseline -> Irrelevant docs flood in again -> Links break.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: 3-5 days of pure rework per minor revision at the publishing stage.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Refine Veeva metadata logic to filter matched documents, or script a macro to auto-remove non-locked documents.
prerequisites: IT adjustment of Veeva RIM configuration

frictionPoints.label: FP3
frictionPoints.title: High-Volume Narrative Drafting
frictionPoints.convergence: The sheer scale of patient events (F5) colliding with manual, stylistic writing processes creates a massive, non-scientific time sink.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Generative AI extraction and summarization of CIOMS/Safety data into standard narrative templates.

frictionPoints.label: FP4
frictionPoints.title: Reviewer Orchestration Drag
frictionPoints.convergence: Cultural habit of ignoring Veeva tasks (F4) forces Medical Writers to act as secretaries, manually tracking 24-30 reviewers across email and Teams.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: Reviewers finish but don't click approve -> MW acts as secretary checking system -> MW sends email/Teams nudge -> Waits -> Repeats until unlocked.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Wasted MW capacity focused on administrative chasing instead of scientific synthesis.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Auto-Orchestrator syncing Route Map with Veeva states.
prerequisites: API access to Veeva workflow states

frictionPoints.label: FP5
frictionPoints.title: Fragmented External Data Handoffs
frictionPoints.convergence: Reliance on up to 12 different external vendors (F7) running separate IT silos (F9) means data integration is incredibly painful and blocks downstream QC steps.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: Vendor delivers bad PDF -> Rejected -> Waits weeks for vendor to fix -> Vendor fixes -> Delivered again -> Repeat.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Months of cumulative project delay per study.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Direct API integration or bringing subject PDF generation in-house.

frictionPoints.label: FP6
frictionPoints.title: Data Cleaning Crunch
frictionPoints.convergence: Absolute requirement for 100% clean data before lock forces impossible manual query load on CDMs inside a 30-day window.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: CML/DM spots impossible data -> Raises query to site -> Site takes 5 days to respond (often incorrectly) -> Re-review and re-query loop.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Massive human stress, burn-out, and hard constraints on study pacing.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Agentic AI reading EDC data and auto-drafting logical queries to sites.
prerequisites: Read/Write API access to EDC (e.g., Rave)

frictionPoints.label: FP7
frictionPoints.title: The Redundant Planning Tax (Upstream)
frictionPoints.convergence: Strict governance requires a full Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) for every study, colliding with the reality that phase 1 'cookie-cutter' studies are 90% identical.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: 2-4 weeks of wasted statistician planning time per standard study.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Modular or exempt SAP models for standard studies; AI drafting based on protocol.

frictionPoints.label: FP8
frictionPoints.title: Ad-Hoc Analytics Collision
frictionPoints.convergence: During Results Review, the team requests new combinations of data (in-text tables). This collides with a constrained programming team already delivering 150-200 base outputs.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: Results Review requests new table -> Programmers delay -> MW tries to manually hack tables together -> Errors caught in review -> Another request to programmers.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Significant delays to Draft 1 completion and high risk of transcription errors.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Self-serve, verified table generators for MWs and Statisticians.

frictionPoints.label: FP9
frictionPoints.title: Template Structural Clashes
frictionPoints.convergence: Merck uses a custom internal document structure, while external vendors use the industry-standard ICH E3 structure.
frictionPoints.loopNarrative: External doc comes in (ICH E3) -> DST manually maps to Merck format -> Feedback sent back -> Vendor revises in their format -> DST manually translates again.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Constant administrative overhead for every external document submission.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: Global harmonization to ICH E3 templates.

frictionPoints.label: FP10
frictionPoints.title: Multi-Author Harmonization Drag
frictionPoints.convergence: 24-30 different reviewers working on the same document collides with strict formatting guidelines, resulting in Frankenstein-like drafts and 'Zombie References'.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: Heavy manual editing to fix formatting, zeroes, brackets, and terminology inconsistencies.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: AI-driven Auto-QC for style guide adherence and reference checking.

frictionPoints.label: FP11
frictionPoints.title: The Layman Translation Burden
frictionPoints.convergence: Strict EU transparency regulations require a layman's summary, colliding with the extreme technical density of a 400+ page CSR.
frictionPoints.estimatedCost: 10 to 12 hours of highly specialized time to condense the study into 2-3 pages.
frictionPoints.leveragePoint: LLM summarization agent tuned to an 8th-grade reading level.
prerequisites: Approved prompt templates and finalized CSR PDF

feedbackLoops.label: R1 — The Copy-Paste Rework Cycle
feedbackLoops.description: Late statistical changes require manual re-typing into the CSR body, which invalidates previous QC checks, triggering a full re-QC cycle, which eats up time and delays the document, inviting even more late changes.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Data-to-text live linking so changes automatically update the document and flag only affected sections for Re-QC.

feedbackLoops.label: R2 — RLCP Baseline Reversion Penalty
feedbackLoops.description: Changing the RLCP status to Baseline auto-matches hundreds of irrelevant documents. DST manually deletes them. If a late change occurs, they must revert to Draft, make the change, and return to Baseline—triggering the auto-match flood all over again and breaking links.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Change Veeva configuration to match on tighter classification parameters, or implement a 'delete unlocked' macro.

feedbackLoops.label: R3 — Vendor Iteration Loop
feedbackLoops.description: Outsourced vendors deliver bad data (like broken Subject PDFs). Because they use disparate systems, it takes weeks to communicate the error, wait for their fix, and receive it back—often resulting in a new error, continually pushing back the QC gate.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Bring subject PDF generation in-house directly connected to Polaris, or enforce API-driven quality checks upon ingestion.

feedbackLoops.label: R4 — The Reviewer Chasing Cycle
feedbackLoops.description: Reviewers finish but don't click approve in Veeva. The MW acts as a secretary checking the system, sending an email nudge, waiting, checking again, and repeating until unlocked.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Auto-Orchestrator syncing Route Map with Veeva states to automate chasing.

feedbackLoops.label: R5 — The Ad-Hoc Table Drag
feedbackLoops.description: Results Review requests new tables. Programmers are delayed. MW tries to manually hack tables together. Errors are caught in review, triggering another request to the programmers.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Self-serve, verified table generators for MWs and Statisticians.

feedbackLoops.label: R6 — The Data Cleaning Query Ping-Pong
feedbackLoops.description: CML/DM spots impossible data, raises a query to the site. The site takes 5 days to respond (often fixing it incorrectly), causing the DM to re-review and re-query while the 30-day clock ticks down.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Agentic AI reading EDC data and auto-drafting logical queries directly to sites.

feedbackLoops.label: R7 — The Template Translation Tax
feedbackLoops.description: External vendor sends doc in ICH E3 format. DST manually maps to Merck format. Feedback sent back. Vendor revises in their format. DST manually translates again.
feedbackLoops.breakPoint: Global harmonization to ICH E3 templates.

capabilityProfiles.name: Results Transcriber Agent
capabilityProfiles.description: Agent that reads structured JSON/XML outputs from ADaM/Nexus and automatically populates in-text tables and standard results boilerplate in the CSR Word document, maintaining live links to the source.

capabilityProfiles.name: Narrative Generation Agent
capabilityProfiles.description: LLM-based agent that ingests patient CIOMS forms and safety listings, applies the Merck Style Guide, and generates cohesive, standardized patient safety narratives for appendix inclusion.

capabilityProfiles.name: Review Orchestrator & Chaser
capabilityProfiles.description: Automates the Route Map. Monitors Veeva workflow states and sends automated Teams/Email nudges to reviewers who haven't clicked 'Approve', escalating automatically if SLAs are breached.

capabilityProfiles.name: Auto-QC Verification Agent
capabilityProfiles.description: Agent that scans the final Word document, identifies statistical claims and data points, and cross-references them against the source TLF repository to highlight discrepancies prior to human QC.

capabilityProfiles.name: Protocol-to-Visuals Agent
capabilityProfiles.description: AI agent that reads protocol schedules of assessments (SoA) and auto-generates visual patient flow diagrams and timelines directly into the CSR template.

capabilityProfiles.name: Layman Summarization Agent
capabilityProfiles.description: Agent that ingests the finalized 400+ page CSR and generates a 2-page, 8th-grade reading level summary for mandatory patient transparency disclosure.

capabilityProfiles.name: Data Cleaning & Query Agent
capabilityProfiles.description: Monitors incoming EDC data, applies cross-field logical checks (e.g., hemoglobin limits vs. survival), and automatically drafts site queries to accelerate the 30-day DB lock crunch.
